Q3 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A queue is implemented using two stack such that ENQUEUE and DEQUEUE operations are performed efficiently. Which one of the following statements is CORRECT (n refers to the number of items in the queue)?

Correct answer: B, A
Solution

(b)
ENQUEUE operation will take Ο(1) time.
DEQUEUE operation will take Ο(n) time for first element after that each element will take Ο(1)
time.

Q6 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statement/statements is/are true?

Correct answer: D, A, B, C
Solution

(d)
(a) False: Ternary search takes more time when compared to binary search.
(b) False: Strassen’s matrix multiplication is a divide and conquer technique.
(c) False: Randomized quick sort handles a sorted array better than traditional quick sort.

Q23 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following options are correct with respect to the following statement.
S1 : A depth-first search of a directed graph always produces the same number of tree edges (i.e. independent of the order in which the vertices are provided and independent of the order of the adjacency lists).
S2 : If a topological sort exists for the vertices in a directed graph, then a DFS on the graph will produce no back edges.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
S1 is False. The DFS forest may contain different numbers of trees (and tree edges) depending on the starting vertex and upon the order in which vertices are searched.
Consider the example below. If the DFS starts at a, then it will visit b next, and (a, b) will become a tree edge. But if the DFS visits b first, then a and b become separate trees in the DFS forest, and (a, b) becomes a cross edge.
S2 is True. Both parts of the statement hold if and only if the graph is acyclic.

higher priority than its children. Following keys are to be inserted,

Note: Key [k, i] depicts ‘i’ is the priority of ‘k’ key. Assume 1 to be the highest priority.
The number of swaps required are _______.

NAT
Correct answer: 5
Solution

5
